# Transcrição: 27. Get Rid of Government 

- **Fonte:** 27. Get Rid of Government 
- **Duração:** 01:50
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:50]

Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. Well, look, I'm not too happy today because I feel like I'm behind bars because Congress capitulated to the Senate. Senate keeps outplaying these guys. The Democrats keep putting the Republicans in a position where they have to freaking bail. I feel like I'm behind bars, man. Look, this is what we ought to do. Take the Senate and the Congress. Everybody that has voted on going over budget, red ink, more of the same-o-same-o, put these guys behind these bars. Let me out, man. We're walking into 2012 with the same problems that we had in 08, 09, 10, and 11, three years of recession and nobody's staying up to do something different. America, raise up. How about a common-sense party rather than Republicans and Democrats? How about a common-sense party to get back to being a free country where man has free rights, he's free to go out and bust out into the marketplace, get what he wants, gets whatever share he feels like is his fair share? Look, anybody that I know that is a true American just wants their opportunity, government, get out of the way, Congress and Senate, you don't do anything right. Keep them uninvolved in all activities. They don't help America. They don't help Americans and they don't help us make a stronger, greater, more prosperous America. So this is what I'm going to do in 2012, baby. I'm busting out. I'm going to bust out hard and heavy. I'm bringing all the artillery, all the doingness, the best-can-do attitude, all my technology, all the techniques, all the persistence and tenacity necessary for me to make sure that I can take care of myself, my family, my business, and create my own economy and I hope you do the same. So, Congress and Senate, Republican and Democrat, you guys need to get out of the way.
